title: Social Media Manager (Video-First) — AI Venture Studio | Growth Role | Long-Term
description:
ABOUT US



Plaiground is an AI-powered venture studio. We build, launch, and scale AI products — fast. We're not a typical company. We move like a startup, think like investors, and build like engineers. Every product we launch is designed to hit $50k–$100k/month.



We're building something called CLOUT — a personal brand growth system that automates the entire content workflow: research, hooks, scripting, editing, and posting. Think of it as the operating system for creators who want to grow without burning out.



We need someone who lives and breathes this world to help us build it from the inside.



THE ROLE



You'll be the first social media hire at Plaiground. You'll manage and grow our presence on YouTube, Instagram, and TikTok — but more importantly, you'll be the person who helps us understand what a world-class content workflow actually looks like from the inside.



This is not a "post 3 times a week and report metrics" job. This is a builder role. You'll be working directly with the founder, shaping the content strategy, and eventually managing a team of AI agents and humans under you.



If you're great, this becomes a Head of Marketing role. We're serious about that.



WHAT YOU'LL DO



Own the end-to-end content workflow: ideation ? scripting ? editing ? posting ? analysis

Research YouTube trends, hooks, and angles that actually convert to views and subscribers

Write and test hooks for short-form (Reels, Shorts, TikTok) and long-form (YouTube)

Edit videos — clean, punchy, retention-optimised (you know what a good cut feels like)

Post and schedule content across platforms

Track what's working and iterate fast

Help us document and systematise the workflow so we can eventually automate it with AI

Grow into managing the full marketing function as we scale





WHO YOU ARE



You understand what makes a video hook stop the scroll — and you can write them

You know the difference between a good angle and a great one

You've grown (or helped grow) a YouTube channel or social account from scratch

You edit video well — Premiere, CapCut, DaVinci, whatever — you're fast and your output looks professional

You understand the algorithm: YouTube, Instagram Reels, TikTok — you know how they work

You're obsessed with content that performs, not just content that looks nice

You're proactive — you bring ideas, you don't wait to be told what to post

You're comfortable working in a fast-moving, AI-first environment

You want to grow. You're not looking for a job — you're looking for a career



NICE TO HAVE



Experience with AI tools (ChatGPT, ElevenLabs, Descript, Opus Clip, etc.)

Experience working with a personal brand or founder-led content

Understanding of content funnels and how organic content drives business outcomes

Experience with YouTube SEO and thumbnail strategy



TEST PROJECT (REQUIRED TO APPLY)



Before we move forward, we want to see how you think. For Plaiground (plaiground.co — an AI venture studio), please submit:



Two long-form YouTube video title ideas (with a 1-sentence explanation of the angle)

Two short-form hook ideas (opening line or visual hook for a Reel/Short/TikTok)



These should feel native to the AI/startup/founder space. Show us you understand the audience.



Send your test project + a short intro about yourself and any relevant work/channels you've managed.



DETAILS



Full-time, remote

Based in the Philippines preferred

Competitive salary based on experience

Long-term role with real growth potential

Direct access to the founder from day one

title: YouTube Thumbnail Designer - Psychology and Performance Focused
description:
Hi,



We're looking for a YouTube thumbnail designer to join our youtube automation team who understands that beautiful is not always effective.



What we actually care about:



You need to understand the psychology behind why people click. This means:

- Understanding negativity bias, curiosity gaps, and visual/verbal triggers for the specific target audience

- Knowing what makes someone stop scrolling and click

- Being able to create a thumbnail that WORKS, not just "looks good"



What the work looks like:



We run multiple faceless YouTube channels across different niches (history, nostalgia, vehicles, lifestyle and more). You'll be designing thumbnails across several niches at once — not just one channel. We'll brief you on each niche's target audience and provide reference thumbnails. Turnaround is 1 thumbnail per video, delivered 1 day before the scheduled upload.



What you need to know:



Technical Skills:

AI tools (Nano Banana 2, Seedream, etc) - you should know how to use AI to your advantage. We won’t care if your thumbnail is AI generated/half AI generated as long as it works. Access to these paid AI tools will be provided to you

Photoshop (or any other editing software you're comfortable working with) - solid editing skills to refine and composite AI-generated elements (for example add text)

Visual composition - understanding spacing, hierarchy, color psychology, and what actually draws the eye



The Right Mindset:

- You're okay with "simple" thumbnails if they perform better

- You study what works on YouTube, not just what looks pretty

- You can articulate your design decisions based on click-through psychology

- You're data-driven and willing to test different approaches



When you apply please send your portfolio of thumbnails you've created.

Also, expect to complete a small sample task after you apply.



We're not looking for a traditional graphic designer. We're looking for someone who gets the game.

title: Executive Assistant to CEO
description:
Position Overview:



As the Executive Assistant to the CEO, you will play a pivotal role in ensuring the smooth and efficient functioning of the executive office. Your primary responsibility will be to provide comprehensive administrative support to the CEO, managing their schedule, and coordinating various tasks to enhance productivity. Your ability to maintain confidentiality, exercise sound judgment, and proactively handle administrative duties will contribute to the overall effectiveness of the CEO's office.





Pay Range: $8 per hour with a performance review scheduled after 90 days of employment. We believe in rewarding exceptional talents.





About Power Plus Systems:



Power Plus Systems LLC has been a dominant force in the residential electrical contracting industry for two decades, and we continue to lead the way. Our vision is to successfully wire 28,000 homes to completion by the year 2029, ensuring safe and efficient electrical solutions, thereby making a positive impact on the lives of our customers and communities. At Power Plus Systems, LLC, a position with us isn't just a job; it's an opportunity to grow and develop within a company that genuinely appreciates your contributions and dedication to our shared mission.





Objectives:

• Manage and organize the CEO's daily schedule, including meetings, appointments, and travel arrangements.

• Act as a gatekeeper, screening and prioritizing communications to the CEO.

• Coordinate and facilitate communication between the CEO and internal/external stakeholders.

• Anticipate the CEO's needs and proactively handle tasks to ensure optimal time management.

• Coordinate special projects and initiatives as assigned by the CEO.





Competencies:

• Exceptional organizational and multitasking skills, with keen attention to detail.

• Strong written and verbal communication skills.

• Ability to maintain confidentiality and exercise discretion in handling sensitive information.

• Proficient in using Microsoft Office and Google Suites.

• Familiarity with scheduling software; experience with additional productivity tools is an advantage.

• Strong interpersonal skills with the ability to interact effectively with individuals at all levels.

• Proactive problem solver and resourceful thinker.





Equipment Requirements:

• Must own a reliable computer or laptop capable of handling administrative tasks efficiently.

• Must own and actively use an iPhone and/or MacBook or Mac computer for efficient communication and task management.

• High-speed internet connection to support video calls, cloud-based collaboration, and remote work.





Physical Requirements:

• Extended periods of sitting at a desk and working on a computer.



Benefits:

• Growth opportunities in a well-established company.

• Ongoing training and professional development opportunities.

• Opportunity for advancement within the company.

• A supportive and collaborative work environment.

• Company-observed paid United States holidays (full time).

• Paid vacation.





How to Apply:



If you are ready to join Power Plus Systems, LLC, a well-established residential electrical contractor that values its tea
mbers and offers excellent benefits, please submit your application through the link below.




/internationalcareers



Power Plus Systems, LLC is an equal opportunity employer and welcomes applicants from all backgrounds to apply.



Join us at Power Plus Systems, LLC, and be part of our continued success as we embark on the next 20 years of excellence in residential electrical contracting!

description:

title: (1) Full-Time Video Editor & Graphic Designer (AI Tools + Marketing Experience)
description:
OB TITLE:

Full-Time Video Editor & Graphic Designer (AI Tools + Marketing Experience)



ABOUT THE ROLE:

We're a digital marketing agency that works with service-based businesses (medspas, home services, fitness, legal, etc.), primarily in the US market. We're looking for a full-time Video Editor & Graphic Designer who can create scroll-stopping content across multiple formats.



You'll be our go-to creative person — editing short-form videos for social media, producing ad creatives for Meta and Google campaigns, and designing graphics for our clients' brands. You'll also work with AI tools like HeyGen and ElevenLabs to produce AI-generated video and voiceover content.



This isn't a "just cut clips together" role. You'll be creating content that directly drives leads and sales for real businesses, so you need to understand what makes people stop scrolling, click, and take action.



WHAT YOU'LL DO:

-Edit short-form video content (Reels, TikToks, YouTube Shorts) — fast-paced, hook-driven, optimized for engagement

-Create video ads for Meta and Google campaigns (direct response style)

-Produce client deliverables such as testimonial videos, promo videos, and brand content

-Use AI video/voice tools (HeyGen, ElevenLabs) to create and enhance content

-Design static graphics — social media posts, ad creatives, thumbnails, banners

-Collaborate with our team on content strategy and creative direction

-Maintain consistent quality and meet turnaround deadlines



REQUIREMENTS:

-At least 2 years of experience in video editing (Premiere Pro, CapCut, DaVinci Resolve, or similar)

-Strong graphic design skills (Canva Pro at minimum; Photoshop/Illustrator is a plus)

-Experience editing short-form, direct-response, or social media content (not just vlogs or cinematic edits)

-Familiarity with AI content tools — HeyGen, ElevenLabs, or similar platforms

-Good eye for typography, color, and layout

-Understands hooks, pacing, and what performs well on social platforms

-Reliable internet connection and able to work during reasonable overlap hours

-Can manage multiple projects and hit deadlines without constant follow-up



NICE TO HAVE (Not Required, But a Big Plus):

-Experience designing or building landing pages (using tools like GoHighLevel, WordPress, Unbounce, or similar)

-Background working in or with a digital marketing agency

-Basic understanding of paid ads and what makes a creative convert

-Motion graphics or animation skills



COMPENSATION:

-Full-time position

-Salary is open and negotiable based on your experience and skill level

-Long-term role with room to grow as the agency scales



HOW TO APPLY:



Important: To make sure you actually read this post, please start your application with the word "Creative" — applications that don't include this will be skipped.



In your application, include:



-A brief intro about yourself and your relevant experience

-Links to your portfolio or samples of your work (video edits, graphics, landing pages if applicable)

-Which AI tools you've used and how you've used them

-Your expected monthly salary (in USD)

-Your availability to start



We review every application that follows these instructions. Looking forward to hearing from you.

title: Bookkeeper - Xero - Australian Business
description:
We’re a growing digital marketing agency looking for a reliable, detail-oriented bookkeeper to manage our Xero file on an ongoing monthly basis.



This is a remote, long-term role focused on keeping our books accurate, compliant, and clean — not just data entry.



About the Role



You’ll be responsible for maintaining our Xero account, ensuring all transactions are correctly coded, reconciled, and aligned with Australian GST requirements.



This role suits someone who understands Xero well, can think critically, and takes ownership of accuracy.



Key Responsibilities



Weekly bank and credit card reconciliation (Xero)

Monthly financial close and review

Correct GST coding across all transactions

(including overseas SaaS subscriptions and mixed GST scenarios)

Accounts payable (contractors, subscriptions, tools)

Light accounts receivable support

Preparation of data for BAS (not lodgement)

Maintaining a clean and consistent Chart of Accounts

Identifying and correcting mis-coded or duplicate transactions



Must-Have Requirements



Strong experience using Xero (ESSENTIAL)

Solid understanding of Australian GST and BAS requirements

Experience working with service-based businesses or agencies

High attention to detail and accuracy



Nice to Have



Experience with overseas SaaS subscriptions and GST treatment

Familiarity with USD transactions and foreign exchange

Experience with digital agencies or subscription-heavy businesses

Basic financial reporting experience

Ability to identify issues and fix them proactively



Engagement Details



Remote role

Approx. 6–10 hours per month

Ongoing work with potential to scale

Flexible working hours

title: Amazon Online Arbitrage Sourcer (VA) – EU Markets | Remote
description:
We are an established Amazon FBA business based in Germany and are currently expanding our sourcing team.



We are looking for an experienced Online Arbitrage Sourcer who can consistently find profitable deals for Amazon FBA.



Our focus is Shop-to-Amazon sourcing (no Amazon flips) across multiple EU marketplaces.



Responsibilities



Deal Sourcing (Main Focus)

– Find profitable online arbitrage deals for Amazon FBA

– Focus on sourcing from German online shops

– Identify additional opportunities from EU retailers with net purchasing options (e.g. Alza, Proshop, etc.)

– Actively source deals from newsletter promotions and special shop campaigns

– Analyze products using Keepa charts and SellerAmp SAS

– Use sourcing tools like Profitpath

– Calculate profit, ROI, and sales potential

– Provide structured and high-quality product leads



Marketplaces



We are actively selling on:

– Amazon Germany

– Amazon Italy

– Amazon Spain

– Amazon France



Deal Requirements



– Minimum profit per unit: €6–8

– Minimum margin: 12%

– Minimum estimated monthly sales: 25 units

– We prefer fewer, high-quality deals over a large quantity of low-quality deals



Requirements



– Proven experience in Amazon Online Arbitrage sourcing (this is required)

– Experience with EU marketplaces (DE, FR, IT, ES) is mandatory

– Strong knowledge of Keepa (chart analysis is essential)

– Experience with SellerAmp SAS / BuyBot Pro / ProfitGO is required

– Understanding of ROI, profit calculations, BSR, and Buy Box behavior

– Ability to work independently and make data-driven decisions

– Detail-oriented and organized



Nice to Have



– Experience with 
 deal groups

– Understanding of FBA fees and product restrictions

– Experience with sourcing tools like Profitpath



Compensation



– Competitive salary depending on experience

– Performance-based bonuses for profitable deals we purchase and sell

– Salary increases based on strong and consistent performance



Benefits



– Work with an experienced and active Amazon FBA team

– Clear sourcing criteria and structured workflows (no guessing)

– Access to premium tools (Keepa, SellerAmp, Profitpath)

– Long-term collaboration with growth potential

– Fair employer with real opportunity to grow financially

– Performance-driven environment with real scaling opportunities



Working Hours



– Preferred working hours: 8:00 – 17:00 (German time)

– Fully remote position



Trial Phase



– If both sides see a good fit, we will start with a 3–4 day paid trial period

– This allows both you and us to evaluate the collaboration



What We Expect



We are not looking for beginners.

We need someone who can consistently deliver profitable deals and understands the Amazon FBA ecosystem.



How to Apply



Please include:

– Your experience with Online Arbitrage

– Tools you have used

– Examples of deals you have sourced

– Your expected salary



We are looking for a long-term partner who wants to grow with us.

title: Digital Marketing & Campaign Lead
description:
We are a long-standing Singapore home appliance brand looking for a proactive Digital Marketing & TikTok Shop Campaign Lead to drive brand growth, grow our TikTok Shop sales and following, and support online sales through strong planning, coordination, and execution.



This is primarily a planning and coordination role, not a content creation or video editing role. You will take ownership of our social media calendar, EDM planning,
 channel, TikTok Shop affiliate growth, campaign launches, KOL outreach, and cross-department coordination, while working closely with our in-house design team and other stakeholders to ensure campaigns and marketing initiatives are delivered well and on time.



Key Responsibilities

Own and manage the brand’s social media calendar across relevant platforms

Plan EDM strategy, campaign flow, and monthly/weekly sends

Manage and grow our
 channel

Propose fresh marketing ideas and new initiatives to improve GMV and brand image

Plan and coordinate product launch campaigns and ad hoc marketing pushes

Search for, identify, and coordinate TikTok Shop affiliate creators

Build and grow the brand’s TikTok Shop affiliate creator network

Drive TikTok Shop growth in both sales and follower count

Lead KOL, creator, affiliate, and brand partnership outreach and planning

Plan and execute giveaway and community engagement campaigns

Work closely with our in-house designers to brief, review, and deliver campaign assets on time

Coordinate across departments to ensure smooth execution of campaigns and promotions

Track timelines, follow up on deliverables, and keep projects moving



E-commerce Responsibilities

Create and update product listings across relevant e-commerce platforms

Ensure stock information is accurate and updated

Set up and coordinate sale campaigns, bundles, and promotional mechanics

Support TikTok Shop operations, including affiliate coordination and campaign execution

Relay orders and POs clearly to the warehouse team for fulfilment coordination

Support TikTok livestream setup and planning where needed



What We’re Looking For

Strong written and spoken English

Strong planning and project coordination skills

Good sense of branding, marketing, and visual direction

Experience in digital marketing, campaign planning, and EDM management

Experience in TikTok Shop operations and affiliate creator coordination

Comfortable working across departments and following up with different stakeholders

title: Ecommerce Customer Experience Expert
description:
We're growing fast. Nashie (
) is in a significant growth phase, and we're building the team that's going to take us to the next level. This isn't just another customer service role. 



If that excites you, keep reading.



What this role actually is:



You'll be the heartbeat of our customer experience, resolving issues, managing orders, keeping operations running smoothly, and making sure every customer interaction reflects the best of who we are as a brand.



But here's what makes this different: we're an e-commerce business that genuinely embraces AI and smarter ways of working. You won't just be closing tickets, you'll be helping shape how we operate as we scale. Customer experience is the frontline to product & business improvement. We'll rely on you to share insights that will genuinely change our course of direction over time. We're a small time, and everyone's input matters.



You're the right person if...



-You have deep experience in Ecommerce Customer Service with Gorgias and Shopify (3-5 Years Experience). 

-You're obsessed with the customer experience and take genuine pride in getting it right every time

-You live on the cutting edge of AI, you're already using it daily, creatively, and well beyond just writing
s

-You're systems-curious, you see a process and immediately wonder how it could be better



What you'll be doing



-Delivering exceptional customer support across our customer service platform, Gorgias. Owning the queue, driving fast response times, and closing tickets with care and efficiency

-Leading and supporting our offshore customer service team to ensure they're hitting their KPIs, maintaining quality standards, and staying aligned with how we operate.

-Continuously reviewing and improving our systems, automations, and AI tools to reduce response times, increase ticket deflection, and lift overall customer satisfaction

-Capturing and translating customer feedback into clear, actionable insights for the wider team. Feeding into product improvements, marketing messaging, and shipping and fulfilment experience.



Include a short note on how you're currently using AI in your day-to-day life. It doesn't have to be work-related, we're just genuinely curious.

title: Outbound Appointment Setter / Sales Development Representative
description:
About the Role

We’re looking for a high-energy, results-driven Outbound Appointment Setter / SDR to help us connect with qualified leads, book appointments, and drive business growth through proactive outreach.



This role is ideal for someone who:

Enjoys being on the phone

Can build trust quickly

Thrives in a performance-based environment

We’re not just looking for someone who can dial we want someone with a hunter mentality who enjoys the challenge of outbound sales and knows how to turn conversations into opportunities.



What You’ll Do

Book qualified appointments for two growing companies

Make high-volume outbound calls (warm & cold leads)

Book qualified appointments for the sales team

Follow proven scripts, frameworks, and onboarding training

Handle objections confidently and guide conversations toward booking

Update lead statuses and manage pipelines in CRM

Participate in role-plays, coaching, and ongoing sales training

Learn and adapt quickly to our offer, audience, and messaging

(Optional growth) Assist with inbox management and outreach tasks



What We’re Looking For

Must-Have Qualities

Proven outbound calling or appointment setting experience

Strong English communication skills

Confident, persuasive, and emotionally intelligent

Ability to build rapport quickly and reduce resistance

Comfortable with performance-based compensation

Coachable and open to feedback and training

Self-motivated and consistent with daily targets



Preferred Background

Experience making 150–200 calls/day

Background in cold calling, SDR, telesales, or appointment setting

Familiar with CRM tools and pipeline management

Experience in B2B, service-based businesses, coaching, or healthcare (a plus)

Willingness to learn social media outreach and inbox engagement



What Success Looks Like

Success in this role is measured by results, not just activity. You will be expected to:

Maintain strong daily call volume

Consistently book qualified appointments

Improve show-up rates

Contribute to closed deals through quality conversations and follow-ups

We’re looking for someone who can quickly ramp up and help increase qualified conversations week over week.



Base Pay

$5.00 USD per hour

Plus commission on every attended appointment and closed deal.

title: E-Commerce Affiliate Manager
description:
About the Role

KittySpout is looking for an E-Commerce Affiliate Manager to join our team!



In your application/subject message to us put the word "KITTY" or your application will be denied



This role reports directly to our Partnerships & Social Media Manager. We need someone to

own the operational engine behind our product seeding, affiliate program, and partnership

outreach — you’ll be the person who makes sure creators get product in their hands, affiliate

links are live and tracked, invoices and admin are handled, and the program actually grows and

generates more revenue. That is the main goal of this role.



This isn’t a small portfolio role. We’re managing hundreds of active creator relationships across

KittySpout and our newest brand, DoggySpout, with plans to scale well beyond that. You’ll be

doing outreach to 300+ creators per month, managing affiliate relationships at scale, partnering

with media outlets and publications, and reporting on what’s actually converting. If you’re

organized, fast, and excited about building out a high-volume creator and affiliate program at a

brand that’s already doing $22M+ in revenue — this is your role.



Who We Are

KittySpout is a premium DTC brand selling stainless steel cat water fountains, filters, and

accessories. We sell across Shopify, Amazon, and Chewy, and we’re one of the fastest-growing

brands in the pet space — $22M+ in revenue and targeting $45–50M in 2026. We recently

launched DoggySpout, expanding into an entirely new vertical and customer base. We’re lean,

we move fast, and every person on this team has a massive impact on the business.



Culture Fit

Culture-fit is our #1 factor when hiring.

We are a fast-moving, data-driven team with no corporate politics. Everyone is hands-on, and if

something needs doing, you do it — regardless of whether it’s “in your job description.” We

value speed, ownership, and people who overdeliver without being asked. This is not a 9–5

corporate role. If you need rigid processes and someone telling you what to do every day, this is

not the right fit.



What you get: you’ll work directly with our Partnerships Manager and have a direct line to the

founder. You’ll play a central role in scaling one of our highest-leverage growth channels —

creator partnerships and affiliates. We hire people we trust and get out of their way. If you’re the

kind of person who thrives with freedom, accountability, and the ability to make a real impact —

you’ll love it here.



Our core values: Work Smart. Overdeliver. Speed is King. Better Every Day.



Key Objectives

• Own creator outreach for product seeding at high volume — identifying, vetting, and

contacting 300+ creators per month across Instagram, TikTok, and YouTube to get

KittySpout and DoggySpout products into the right hands.

• Set up and manage affiliate links and commission tracking through platforms like

Refersion or Social Snowball — ensuring every creator has a working link and payouts

are accurate.

• Manage the full creator relationship lifecycle after seeding — follow-ups, content

reminders, reactivation of inactive creators, and escalation of top performers for deeper

partnerships. You’ll be managing 300+ active relationships and scaling toward 500+.

• Go beyond creators — proactively pursue partnerships with media outlets, publications,

blogs, and other affiliate channels to drive new distribution and revenue. Think Forbes

placements, niche pet publications, and high-authority sites, not just influencers.

• Handle all invoicing and administrative work with creators and affiliates — commission

payouts, contracts, payment tracking, and any creator-facing admin so nothing slows

down the program.

• Deliver weekly reporting on creator posts, affiliate conversions, media partnership

performance, and seeding pipeline status — so the team always knows what’s working

and what’s not.

• Support whitelisting logistics by coordinating ad account access between creators and

the media buying team, ensuring smooth handoffs for paid amplification.

• Maintain and organize the creator and partner database — tracking outreach status,

content delivery, performance metrics, and relationship notes in a clean, accessible

system.

• Proactively identify high-performing creators and flag them for deeper investment — paid

partnerships, ambassador programs, or exclusive collaborations. Bring new ideas for

affiliate growth channels beyond the standard playbook.



Requirements

• Fully remote position (must be available for overlap with US Eastern timezone core

hours).

• Fluent in English (written and verbal) — you’ll be writing outreach messages and

communicating with creators daily.

• 2–4 years of experience in influencer marketing, affiliate management, or creator

partnerships at a DTC brand or influencer/affiliate agency. You need to understand the

difference between seeding and paid placement — and know how to run both.

• Hands-on experience with affiliate platforms like Refersion, Social Snowball, Impact, or

similar tools for link generation, commission tracking, and payout management.

• Familiarity with creator discovery and tracking tools like MightyScout, Modash,

CreatorIQ, or similar — or a proven manual outreach process that gets results at scale.

• Able to manage 300+ active creator and partner relationships simultaneously without

dropping balls — and ready to scale that to 500+. You need a system, not just a

spreadsheet you forget to update.

• Strong written communication skills — your outreach messages need to feel personal

and specific, not copy-pasted templates. Creators can tell the difference.

• Extremely organized and proactive — you flag problems before they escalate and you

don’t wait to be told what to do next.

• Comfortable working in a fast-paced, lean environment where processes are still being

built. You need to thrive in ambiguity, not freeze.

• Aligned with our values: work smart, overdeliver, speed is king, better every day. Willing

to go above and beyond, including outside standard hours when the business needs it.



Accountabilities

What success looks like in this role:

• 300+ new creator and partner outreach messages sent per month, with a target

response rate tracked and improved over time. Volume scales as we grow — the

expectation is high output from day one.

• 100% of seeded creators have working affiliate links within 48 hours of product delivery

confirmation.

• 300+ active creator and partner relationships managed with zero dropped balls —

scaling toward 500+ as DoggySpout ramps.

• Weekly seeding and affiliate performance report delivered to the Partnerships Manager

— covering outreach volume, response rates, content posts, affiliate conversions, and

media partnership progress.

• All creator and affiliate invoices, payouts, and admin processed accurately and on time

— no payment delays, no missing paperwork.

• Creator and partner database maintained in real-time — no outdated statuses, no

missing follow-ups, no relationships falling through the cracks.

• Whitelisting requests coordinated and completed within 24 hours of approval, with zero

missed handoffs to the media buying team.

• Monthly analysis of top-performing creators and partners with recommendations for

deeper investment or scaling — delivered by the 5th of each month.

• Zero seeded products sent without proper tracking and follow-up in place. Every unit we

ship to a creator should have a clear path to content and conversion.



How Our Hiring Works

We move fast and respect your time. Our process has three stages: (1) Soft Interview — a

conversational call to understand your background and see if there’s mutual fit. (2) Technical

Interview + Test Project — a deeper dive into your partnerships and affiliate experience plus a

short practical exercise so we can see how you think and work. (3) Culture Fit Interview — a

final conversation with the founder to make sure this is the right team for you and you’re the

right person for us.



Timeline from first interview to offer is typically 8–10 business days. KPIs and performance

targets will be defined together before your start date so expectations are crystal clear from day

one.



Compensation

$30,000 – $45,000 USD per year

Based on experience and expertise. This role includes a performance bonus structure tied to

affiliate program growth and creator output metrics. We invest in the people who earn it — as

the program scales, so does your scope and compensation

To ensure success, Sales Operations Associates should have experience in a sales and/or customer

.

 requests

? Contribute to ongoing process and workflow improvements

? Proactively builds trusting internal and external relationships. Can effectively communicate within all levels with the company, the customer, and vendor

Requirements

title: Senior Accounting Manager
description:
IMPORTANT NOTE: We will only process applicants who will submit their RESUME and a link to their VOICE RECORDING via
 to 
 is a permanent WFH employment. All government-mandated benefits are provided. We will only interview applicants who have experience. Please do not apply if you do not have a Data Center and IT industry experience.





Job Summary

The Senior Accounting Manager is responsible for managing the day-to-day accounting operations of the company and ensuring the timely execution of core accounting processes. This role will supervise the accounting team and oversee the execution of the monthly close process, reconciliations, and operational accounting workflows.



The Senior Accounting Manager will work closely with the Controller to ensure accounting activities are completed accurately and on schedule. While the Controller retains ownership of financial reporting strategy, technical accounting decisions, and executive financial oversight, this role focuses on operational execution and team management within the accounting function.



The successful candidate will possess excellent attention to detail as well as exceptional communication and analytical skills.  This individual should be comfortable in a “hands on” environment, but possess the skills necessary to grow with the Company.



Position Responsibilities to include:

Manage the day-to-day operations of the accounting department.

Supervise accounting staff responsible for Accounts Payable, Accounts Receivable, payroll processing support, general ledger, and other accounting activities.

Monitor team workload distribution and ensure operational continuity.

Provide coaching, guidance, and performance feedback to accounting tea
mbers.

Ensure accounting tasks and deadlines are executed according to established timelines.

Ensure an accurate and timely monthly, quarterly and year end close. Coordinate cross-functional activities in support of close requirements. Manages journal entries and conducts account reconciliations and analysis.

Review reconciliations and schedules prepared by accounting staff prior to submission to the Controller.

Produce financial statements and analyses in accordance with US GAAP.

Develop and maintain fixed asset sub-ledger.

Coordinate purchasing cycle in accordance with Company policies. 

Provide support to the external CPA firm to ensure a clean and timely year end audit.

Support budget and forecasting activities.

Assist in developing policies and internal control improvements. Recommend workflow enhancements as required.

Understands and follows payroll regulations and labor laws that impact payroll processing, ensures payroll is processed accurately and timely, and collects all necessary supporting documentation and approvals needed.

Prepare and processes the tax filings of the Company

Reviews, analyzes, and presents financial data in response to ad hoc queries from management or regulatory bodies.

 







Position Requirements:

7 to 10+ years of supervisory experience in the accounting/financial reporting areas. Experience in a growing start-up environment a plus

Bachelor’s degree in Accounting, Finance, or relevant work experience, CPA preferred.

Experience working in U.S.-based accounting environments.

Strong knowledge of US GAAP fundamentals.

Experience with ERP systems (NetSuite preferred).

Advanced Excel proficiency.

Experience supporting payroll and financial reporting processes.

Must have experience with cost accounting and possess thorough knowledge of job costing concepts and procedures.

Strong verbal and written communication skills.

Strong people management and team leadership skills.

Excellent interpersonal, supervisory and customer service skills.

Detail-oriented with exceptional analytical, problem solving and prioritization skills.

Ability to multi-task, work under pressure, meet deadlines and be able to thrive in a growth oriented work environment.

description:

 to 
 is a permanent WFH employment. All government-mandated benefits are provided. We will only interview applicants that have experience. Please do not apply if you do not have a Data Center and IT industry experience.



Position Overview

We are looking for a highly skilled, enthusiastic and self-driven Senior Operations Manager to join our team.

This individual will be responsible for managing the day to day operations and ensuring we are meeting the highest standard for customer service, as well as the smooth orchestration of order processing, commitment, and fulfillment.  



The ideal candidate will have a track record of success in a logistics role, with a natural talent for motivating cross functional teams and have a deep understanding of what it takes to build and maintain a trusted relationship with all of your business partners both internal and external. They will have a strong technical and analytical background, as well as the desire to make a significant business impact by owning and driving supply chain efficiencies to enable major business objectives. 



This position will directly oversee the Order Management and Warehouse Team.



Responsibilities



?	Supervises all shipping, receiving, and inventory control activities performed by warehouse associates.

?	Maintains department standards for productivity, reliability, and accuracy.

?	Confers with department heads to coordinate warehouse activities, such as production, sales, records control or purchasing.

?	Reviews invoices, work orders, consumption reports, or demand forecasts to estimate peak delivery periods and to issue work assignments.

?	Achieves warehouse KPI, operation optimization, and cost control goals.

?	Prepares, coordinates, and manages physical inventory, including controlling inventory levels by conducting physical counts and reconciling with ERP (NetSuite).

?	Monitors and manages min/max levels on inventory, making recommendations for fine-tuning inventory levels and mitigating obsolete and/or slow-moving items

?	Achieves financial objectives by preparing an annual budget; scheduling expenditures; analyzing

?	variances; initiating corrective actions.

?	Supervises team to include hiring, training, reviewing and approving time cards, planning and assigning daily work; conducting performance appraisals, addressing performance issues, and resolving problems. 

?	Maintains a customer service-oriented philosophy in all conduct of self and warehouse staff.

?	Ensure the efficient utilization and physical condition of warehousing space by planning and implementation of new design layouts; inspecting equipment; issuing work orders for repair and requisitions for replacement.

?	Performs regular cycle counts to monitor inventory accuracy and creates action plans to address all deficiencies found in the cycle count program.

?	Reviews all shipping, receiving, and inventory discrepancies and determine the root cause.

?	Interacts with customers, suppliers, and transport companies to resolve problems with the delivery of shipments.

?	Negotiates with carriers, warehouse operators, or insurance company representatives for services and preferential rates.

?	Responsible for SOP document release and execution, creating and improving the workflow

?	Attend Quarterly cadence calls with key vendors to better address operational issues

?	Own reviewing all SO’s as final approval step before releasing to have PO’s cut

?	Oversee and handle RMAs

?	Review and Track with Team following:

?	No ISD’s after 2 days

?	ISD is past with no IAD

?	Drop Shipments past ISD without invoices

title: Customer Support Specialist
description:
Job Description - Customer Support (CS) Specialist



Role Summary: You will operate as frontline Tier 1 Support at Tessaract, handling incoming customer requests via Zendesk and Live Chat within defined service levels.



This role is accountable for:

- timely first responses,

- accurate triage and classification (P3/P4 ownership),

- applying approved knowledge base (KB) solutions, and

- preparing structured, escalation-ready handovers to Tier 2 when required.



You are not currently expected to perform deep technical investigation or system changes. Your responsibility is to ensure issues are clarified, documented properly, and either resolved within scope or escalated cleanly without bounce-backs.



This role is remote and Zendesk-based, with clear escalation boundaries and performance standards.



Key Responsibilities:



1. Frontline Support and Triage

- Respond to incoming tickets and Live Chat requests within the defined SLA.

- Clarify the issue by confirming:

   - the client’s request,

   - expected versus actual behaviour,

   - scope of impact (who / what / when).

- Classify tickets accurately (P0 - P4) using defined priority guidelines.

- Own P3 and P4 tickets within permitted scope.



2. Resolution Within Tier 1 Scope

- Apply documented knowledge base (KB) solutions and approved playbooks.

- Provide known workarounds where available.

- Perform light configuration guidance within approved boundaries.

- Collect required diagnostics:

   - screenshots,

   - timestamps,

   - exact error messages,

   - reproduction steps from the user perspective.

- You are not authorised to perform:

   - system configuration changes,

   - data cleanup or corrective fixes,

   - migrations,

   - custom or bespoke work.

- Any such requests must be escalated.



3. Escalation Discipline

- Prepare structured, complete escalation packets for Tier 2, including:

   - reproduction summary,

   - expected vs actual behaviour,

   - impact and priority,

   - relevant evidence and logs (if accessible).

- Ensure no ticket is escalated without the required information.

- Prevent “incomplete escalation” bounce-backs.

- Follow up on escalations to ensure customer communication remains consistent.



4. Customer Communication and Professionalism

- Maintain clear, concise, and professional communication aligned with PDPA and internal standards.

- Set appropriate expectations for response and resolution timelines.

- Ensure customers do not feel “bounced” between teams.

- Escalate dissatisfaction signals early to Team Lead / CS Manager / CS Director.



5. Documentation and Process Discipline

- Maintain clean, accurate ticket documentation.

- Use required Zendesk fields, macros, and tags consistently.

- Contribute to KB improvements when recurring issues are identified.

- Follow SOPs for i
, outages, and billing queries.



6. Requirements

- 1–3 years’ experience in Customer Support, Technical Support, or similar SaaS support role.

- Experience handling ticketing systems (Zendesk or equivalent).

- Strong written communication skills.

- Ability to follow structured playbooks and work independently in a remote environment.

- Comfortable working with defined escalation boundaries.



7. Preferred

- Experience supporting B2B SaaS clients.

- Exposure to workflow-heavy software (legal tech, accounting, ERP).

- Experience working alongside Technical Support and Product teams.

- Familiarity with escalation templates and structured issue management.



8. Working Model

- Remote role.

- 09:00–18:00 SGT coverage (or rostered shifts where applicable).

- Zendesk-first workflow.

- Reports to CS Team Lead.



9. Working Style Expectations

- Clear ownership of assigned tickets.

- Structured, disciplined communication.

- Does not exceed scope boundaries.

- Escalates early when unsure.

- Comfortable saying: “This requires Tier 2 review.”



Why This Role Matters

- Tier 1 Support is the first experience customers have when something goes wrong.

- Strong performance in this role:

   - protects retention,

   - reduces operational drag,

   - prevents unnecessary escalations,

   - and improves overall cost-to-serve.

title: White Label Brand Designer (per project)
description:
YOU CAN LEARN MORE ABOUT THE ROLE/RESPONSIBILITIES AND SUBMIT YOUR APPLICATION AT:





** only applications that follow the directions on the job post link above and submit their CV/resume via the Google Form will be considered. Compensation is negotiable based on your experience.

___



JOB OVERVIEW:



We’re looking for a strategic, detail-oriented Brand Designer to join our team on a white label, per-project basis. This role is perfect for someone who loves building brands from the ground up, thrives in both sprint-style projects and longer timelines, and wants to grow alongside a boutique agency working with health, wellness, and coaching brands.



As our white label Brand Designer, you’ll take brand strategy and creative direction and translate them into complete identity systems (logo suites, typography, color palettes, and brand guidelines, etc.) that capture a client’s essence while setting them up for long-term success. You’ll be responsible for ensuring every asset is intentional, polished, and consistent across all touchpoints.



You won’t need to handle direct client calls; instead, your focus will be on design and execution. Projects are managed asynchronously in Asana/ClickUp, with creative direction provided through Loom and written notes. We run a mix of branding sprints (e.g. Brand in a Week) and larger custom branding projects that span several weeks, so you’ll need to be comfortable working on both compressed timelines and in-depth brand builds.



This role goes beyond simply creating logos. You’ll also be responsible for packaging brand files, creating brand guides, exporting assets, and preparing deliverables for seamless client handoff — helping us uphold the agency’s reputation for high-touch, strategy-driven design.



We see this as more than a contractor role — it’s an opportunity to become a long-term strategic partner. If the collaboration is a great fit, there is room to move into a retainer-based role and eventually take on a Lead Brand Designer position, overseeing brand identity builds from concept to delivery.



ABOUT US:



Brand With Impact is a boutique creative agency co-founded by Angelique Vestil. We partner with health, wellness, and personal development leaders who are out there shaping industries and changing lives. Our work goes beyond making things look good.. Our goal is to build brands and websites that connect deeply with your audience, tell your story with clarity, and are strategically designed to convert.

To support our growth, we’re looking for a white-label Brand Designer who can take full ownership of branding projects — from concept to final delivery — so we can streamline our in-house workflow and better serve our clients.

title: Customer Support Representative for Shopify E-commerce, US business hours
description:
Join a Fast-Growing Premium Coffee Equipment E-commerce Store — Remote Customer Support & Operations

We are a US-based online retailer specializing in premium espresso machines and coffee grinders, carrying world-class brands like Mahlkönig, Rocket Espresso, and Ceado. We're looking for a reliable, detail-oriented customer support and operation agent to join our small, remote team.



Your daily responsibilities:

- Respond to customer inquiries via phone,
 and live chat regarding orders, shipping timelines, and product questions

- Process and track orders with US-based distributors, ensuring timely fulfillment

- Proactively communicate with customers when orders are delayed or out of stock, managing expectations professionally

- Send a daily operations report summarizing order status, open issues, and anything requiring escalation

- Flag potential chargeback risks early and coordinate resolution before they escalate



You'll go far if you're:

- Calm and professional under pressure. Our customers spend $1,000+ per order and expect premium service

- A clear, confident English speaker

- Highly organized and proactive: you spot problems before they become crises

- A fast learner comfortable picking up new tools quickly



Skills required:

- Excellent spoken English (non-negotiable)

- Ability to organize and resolve multiple support tickets

- Comfortable with ecommerce platforms (Shopify experience is a plus)

- Familiar with order tracking and logistics communication



Nice to have:

- Customer service experience for high-ticket products



Work schedule:

- Full-time, remote (8 hours/day)

- US business hours coverage required — Monday to Friday, aligned to US ET time zones (Philippines night shift)



Salary: $700/month



Perks after 6-month probation:

- 13th month bonus

- Cash equivalent for unused leaves



To apply:
 us with the subject line "Crawford Espresso Support Application" and include a brief introduction, relevant work experiences, and a recording of your spoken English.

Angelique Vestil & Brand With Impact is a boutique branding and web design agency for health and wellness practitioners. Think functional medicine doctors, fertility coaches, health clinics, nutritionists, therapists, wellness centers, etc.. Our clients are serious professionals with established practices, and they trust us to build brands that feel elevated, strategic, and completely them.



We're looking for a white label Brand Designer to take full ownership of branding projects, from concept to final delivery, on a per-project basis.



This is a remote, async-first role. You won't be on client calls. You'll receive strategy, creative direction, and written briefs from us and deliver polished, complete brand identity systems. Projects are managed in Asana/Notion with direction shared via Loom and written notes.



WHAT YOU'LL OWN:



- Full brand identity builds: logo suites, typography, color palette, brand guidelines

- All work must be fully custom and original (no AI or logo templates)

- Supporting brand assets as needed per project scope

- Proper file packaging, asset export, and client-ready handoff folders

- Consistency and quality across every single deliverable



YOU'RE THE RIGHT FIT IF:



- You have 3+ years building brand identities for service-based businesses, ideally in health, wellness, coaching, or personal development

- Your portfolio shows range and restraint. You know when to be bold and when to pull back.

- You can work with a brief and run with it without constant check-ins

- You take feedback without ego and revise with precision

- You have a strong process for file organization and delivery

- You're looking for a long-term creative partnership, not just one-off gigs



THIS IS NOT THE RIGHT FIT IF: 



- You need a lot of hand-holding or check-ins to get started

- Your portfolio is mostly logo-only work with no full brand systems

- You're not comfortable with async feedback via Loom or written notes

- You've never designed for a health, wellness, or coaching brand



PROJECT TYPES: 



We run both sprint-style branding projects (Brand in a Week) and longer custom builds spanning several weeks. You need to be comfortable with both.



TOOLS WE USE:



Adobe, Figma, Notion, Asana, Loom, Markup, Canva, Google Drive for deliverables.



GROWTH PATH:



This starts as a per-project contractor role. If the work is consistently excellent and the collaboration flows, this moves into a retainer arrangement and eventually a Lead Brand Designer position, where you'd oversee brand builds from kickoff to delivery.



TO APPLY: 



Fill out the application below. We read every submission carefully. No generic or AI answers please, we can tell.

title: Social Content Assistant
description:
We're looking for (the best) **Social Content Assistant** to join **dayrise**.



If you're into **sports, travel, and digital culture**, thrive on **structure but crave adventure**, and want to work in true **Gen Z style** — this might just be your dream job.



If this role already feels like it was written with you in mind, that's usually a good sign.



---



## The Upside



dayrise is a consulting firm for celebrities, creators, and brands. We define strategy, build positioning frameworks, and engineer long-term growth for a very selective group of talent whose vision aligns with ours. We have been invitation-only from day one.



dayrise was founded in May 2023 and, as of 2026, represents a roster reaching 17 million followers across platforms. Campaigns managed by dayrise have run with Airbnb, IKEA, Magnum, Trade Republic, Visit Dubai, Visit Abu Dhabi, and Skyscanner, among others.



**Our principle is simple: to do well and be well.**



We are now turning that same strategic machine on ourselves. The dayrise brand is launching its own social presence and we need one person to build it. At the same time, the founder is building her personal brand from scratch — deliberately, separately, and with full strategic intent. A business partner is doing the same. This role sits at the centre of all three.



---



## Elevator Pitch



Picture this: you're the creative engine behind a fast-growing consulting firm, a founder scaling her first business from 7 to 8 figures while simultaneously building a new one from scratch, and a business partner running their own company — all at once. You're building content systems, owning social presence across LinkedIn, Instagram, and TikTok, and learning directly from a founder who sits inside the strategy of a 18-million-follower roster. No micromanagement. No rigid 9–5. Just high standards, real ownership, and a performance structure that means the better you do, the more you earn.



If you're the type of person who analyses content in your spare time, has opinions about why certain creators win and others plateau, and is hungry to build something that lasts — this is your room.



And yes, we notice people who pay attention.



---



## Your Role in a Nutshell



- **Build the content engine** — develop a repeatable, platform-native system for producing short-form video, carousels, written content, and visual assets across LinkedIn, Instagram, and TikTok.

- **Own multiple presences** — manage content across several active accounts: the dayrise brand, the founder's personal brand (operating independently), and a business partner's brand. Each has a distinct voice, audience, and goal.

- **Execute two distinct tracks per account** — feed content builds authority and community; non-follower content drives new audiences into the funnel. Both must be managed simultaneously, with different creative logic.

- **Make the numbers move** — set up meaningful KPIs, track what works, and bring the data that shapes what we do next.

- **Run and learn paid advertising** — manage boosted posts and paid campaigns on Instagram and LinkedIn. Dedicated budget will be allocated to both the founder's account and the dayrise brand.



If you naturally read things in order, you'll probably enjoy how we work.



---



## What Success Looks Like



**At 30 days:** You've absorbed both strategies, studied all inspiration accounts, built mood boards and voice documents for every entity, and delivered a content framework and KPI dashboard. You haven't posted much yet. That's correct — the foundation is everything.



**At 90 days:** Content is live, consistent, and performing across all accounts. The two-track system is running. Non-follower reels are bringing in new audiences. Feed content is building trust and community. You're tracking weekly, flagging what's working, and proactively suggesting what to do next.



**At 6 months:** The accounts are growing. Paid campaigns are live and optimised. Leads are arriving pre-educated. LinkedIn is generating real conversation. The founder's personal brand is gaining 
ntum independently. You've earned more than your base through performance bonuses.



---



## Ideal Candidate Profile — Is This You?



- **You're Gen Z** — not as a box-tick, but because native platform fluency cannot be taught. You don't just use these platforms. You understand them at a mechanical level.

- **You're obsessed in the good way** — you study content like others study sport. You reverse-engineer why things work. You have strong opinions about what's lazy and what's great.

- **You're a builder, not a passenger** — you see what needs to happen and you make it happen. You don't need someone to chase you.

- **You bring a creative eye you can back up** — not aesthetic preference. Craft. You can point to work you've made and explain the decisions behind every choice.

- **You understand the difference between feed content and reach content** — two completely different creative briefs. You can execute both without conflating them.

- **You're data-fluent** — you know how to build a tracker, read the numbers, and make recommendations based on them.

- **You can hold multiple voices at once** — creating for a founder, a brand, and a business partner, each with a distinct voice, audience, and tone.

- **You're open to paid advertising** — whether you already know it or are ready to learn, paid ads will be part of this role.

- **You care about doing things properly** — dayrise operates to a high standard. Clarity, precision, and reliability are non-negotiable.

- **You notice details** — in content, in communication, and in the way instructions are given.

- **Your English is exceptional** — perfect written and verbal English is a must. You know how to write with clarity, nuance, tone, and precision across different formats and audiences.

- **You understand English-speaking culture deeply** — especially the humour, references, tone, and social cues that make content feel native rather than translated or generic.



---



## This Is NOT For You If…



- You want a job you can coast in. This role has real expectations and real accountability.

- You're looking for someone to hand you a brief every morning. You bring your own ideas.

- You see social media as a checkbox, not a craft. We can't train that obsession in.

- You need rigid hours and structured sign-off on everything. We care about outcomes, not timesheets.

- You've never made anything. No portfolio, no account, no experiments. We need evidence.

- You're not comfortable being accountable for real results on real accounts with real audiences.

- You tend to skim and improvise instead of reading properly and following through.



---



## What You'll Be Doing



- **Content creation and editing** — end-to-end production of short-form video, carousels, and written posts across LinkedIn, Instagram, and TikTok. Scripting, filming direction, editing, B-roll, captions, and platform-specific formatting. You're producing, not just editing.

- **Two-track content management** — for each account: feed content (authority, community, depth) and non-follower content (reach, virality, funnel entry) as two distinct tracks.

- **Multi-entity management** — the dayrise brand, the founder's personal brand, a business partner's brand, and team profiles. Each entity has its own voice and goal.

- **LinkedIn strategy and outreach** — LinkedIn is our primary B2B lead engine. Daily connection requests (up to 30 per account), deliberate content strategy, conversion-focused follow-up, and daily outreach to potential prospects.

- **Paid advertising** — manage and optimise boosted posts and paid campaigns on Instagram and LinkedIn. Dedicated budget provided.

- **Presentations and infographics** — pitch decks, visual one-pagers, and branded infographics.

- **Performance tracking and reporting** — weekly KPI review per platform, per entity. You own this — it's a decision tool, not a formality.

- **Trend monitoring** — study accounts like 
) and 
) weekly. Flag emerging formats before they peak.

- **Content framework development** — build the repeatable system: rhythm, templates, production process, content calendar.

- **Lead magnets and value assets** — guides, quizzes, and frameworks that generate pre-educated inbound leads.

- **Asset management** — a clean, structured content folder system. No chaos.



A lot of this role comes down to taste, consistency, and noticing what most people miss.



---



## Win-Win Compensation



**Base:** ~€1,200–€1,400/month



**Performance bonuses:**



- Bonus for content that goes viral (threshold defined per platform and account size)

- Bonus per qualified inbound lead generated through content

- Bonus for hitting follower and engagement growth milestones

- Bonus per converted lead attributed to social or paid content



> We want you in the driver's seat. When the content wins, you win. This isn't a vague "we'll reward you" promise — it's a structured incentive we'll define together before you start.

> *Contract: 12-month independent contractor agreement. 2-month trial. Remote-flexible, output-driven.*



---



## Why This Is Different



- **You'll be trained by someone who's actually done it** — Giada Rissotto, founder of dayrise, who has built and advised a 17-million-follower roster and managed campaigns for Airbnb, IKEA, Magnum, and Trade Republic, will give you direct, hands-on mentorship from week one.

- **You'll own real accounts with real stakes** — live accounts, real audiences, real consequences. Not spec work. Not a test. Ownership.

- **You'll build from the beginning** — the dayrise brand is launching now. What you create in the next 12 months will define what we look like for years.

- **You'll learn what most roles never teach** — personal brand architecture, multi-entity content systems, LinkedIn as a lead engine, paid amplification strategy, and the inner workings of a consulting firm at the forefront of the European creator economy.

- **You'll learn how modern brands scale** — by working alongside specialists who turn processes into efficient, AI-powered systems, you'll see first-hand how a growing business becomes more effective and built to scale.

- **Opportunity to grow into leadership** — as we put the right systems and processes in place to build brands more efficiently and at scale, there will be scope to take on more responsibility and eventually help lead the team we hire to do this for more clients.



Some people will read this and think it sounds intense. The right person will read it carefully and smile.



---



## What the Content Actually Looks Like



These are real examples — specifically selected during briefing — of the content directions you'll be executing across all accounts. Study these before you apply. They are not mood board inspiration. They are the brief.



**Giada's account — feed content**



[Lifestyle carousel: nice outfit, grounding caption](
) — High-production imagery, disarmed by a caption that says "I'm just like you." The formula is: aspirational image + self-aware, slightly humorous caption. Every lifestyle carousel follows this.



[Day-in-the-life cinematic series](
) — More cinematic than typical B-roll. Shot like a short film. Gives the series its own visual identity and delivers value by showing *how* Giada operates, not just telling.



**Giada's account — non-follower reels (reach content)**



[Lifestyle reel that drives profile visits](
) — Zero context needed. Someone who's never heard of Giada sees this on Explore and taps the profile. The feed content does the converting. The reel just has to stop the scroll.



[Introduction to what I'm building](
) — Mystery + aspiration + comment CTA mechanic. The audience doesn't know what the second business is yet. That's intentional. The curiosity is the content.



**dayrise account — brand content**



[Story-led, phone-filmed mini-ad](
) — Every major dayrise 
nt gets this treatment. Narrative arc, not announcement. "Here's what happened" is always more powerful than "here's what we do."



[Vision and soul reel — not what we do, but who we are](
) — The most important format for dayrise. No campaigns referenced. No deliverables mentioned. Just the atmosphere of the world we move in: fashion weeks, NBA, football matches, tourism board trips. The feeling of being in the room.



[Office hours as a reality show](
) — Real work 
nts edited with drama and energy. *The Office* meets a talent consultancy. This is how dayrise shows its team without it feeling corporate or staged.



**George's LinkedIn account** — Simple, founder-led content that speaks directly to traditional business owners and shows how AI and better systems make their businesses more efficient, more profitable, and easier to scale. The content sits across three pillars: AI, founder-led businesses, and a selective lifestyle that resonates with business owners in their 40s, 50s, and 60s. It includes sharing insights, breakthroughs, and case studies; teaching business owners what (not how) AI can actually do for them; and examples of how we have helped.F



If you got this far, you probably already understand why we like people who read properly.



---



## How to Apply



Don't send a CV. Send us this instead, in the exact order below:



1. A link to something you've made that you're proud of — and two sentences on why it worked (or why it didn't).

2. One creator you think is doing it better than everyone else right now — and specifically what they're doing that others aren't.

3. What excites you most about this role — and what makes you slightly nervous. Honesty is a green flag.

4. One content idea — a format, series concept, or platform experiment — that you think we should try.



Please use bullet points, not paragraphs. Start your
 with "Dear Gaia" and include one final sentence on what you believe your X-factor is. Somewhere in your
, include the word ORBIT and a ?.



Send to 
 with the subject line: *I want in.*

title: Fullstack .NET Developer
description:

You’ll be working directly on a 
-based architecture using Blazor WebAssembly, a BFF pattern, and a PostgreSQL-backed API.



About the Role

Building features across 
 backend and Blazor frontend

Collaborating on improvements to system architecture and performance

 6+ (ASP.NET Core APIs)

What You’ll Be Doing

Extend and 
 APIs

Working With Us

Opportunity to shape how the system evolves

title: Virtual Assistant / Appointment Setter
description:
READ BEFORE APPLYING – MOST APPLICATIONS WILL BE IGNORED



We are hiring a Virtual Assistant for a fast-paced U.S.-based home service company.



This is a serious, long-term role. This is not a backup job or something to “try out.”



If you are unreliable, late, disappear during shifts, or do not follow instructions, do not apply.



DO NOT APPLY IF



Your English is not clear and confident

You are not comfortable speaking on the phone all day

You are slow, disorganized, or easily distracted

You have a history of disappearing from jobs

You are looking for something easy



REQUIREMENTS



Strong English with minimal accent

Stable and reliable internet

Backup power

Quiet workspace with no background noise

Professional headset



MANDATORY VOICE RECORDING – NO EXCEPTIONS



If you do not submit a voice recording, your application will not be reviewed.



This role is phone-heavy. Your voice and communication skills are critical.



RECORDING RULES



Record in a quiet environment

Use a headset (not speaker or phone mic)

Speak clearly, naturally, and confidently

Do not use AI or voice tools



SCRIPT – READ EXACTLY



Hello, this is (your name) calling with Professional Garage Door Services.



Our phone number is
, that’s
.



The reason I’m calling is because you recently submitted an inquiry regarding garage door assistance, and I’d like to learn a little more about the issue you’re experiencing and how we can help.



We specialize in garage door repairs, maintenance, and new installations, and our technicians can handle most issues the same day.



We actually have a technician working nearby, so we may be able to provide same-day service depending on availability.

title: Survey Fielding Manager – Opinion Research
description:
FOR APPLICANTS WITH HANDS-ON SURVEY PROGRAMMING AND FIELDING EXPERIENCE IN PUBLIC OPINION OR MARKET RESEARCH



Remote, (Eastern Time Zone)



Who You Are

You program survey instruments, manage the field, clean the data, coordinate with vendors, and keep projects moving on schedule. You've done this work hands-on at a research firm (not in an academic setting), and you're comfortable owning the technical execution of multiple projects at once.



You may be a fit if:

* You have programmed surveys in Alchemer, Qualtrics, or a comparable professional survey platform - and can set up skip logic, quotas, randomization, piping, and multilanguage surveys without hand-holding

* You've managed survey fielding from launch through close: monitoring quotas, tracking sample quality, and keeping projects on timeline

* You've coordinated with external vendors - panel providers, call centers, mail houses - and can manage those relationships independently

* You have experience with sample preparation: lists manipulation, deduplication, creating clean sample files, generating unique identifiers

* You are meticulous about data quality - you catch errors before they reach anyone else

* You take initiative, ask questions when something is unclear, and don't wait to be told what to do next

* You're comfortable jumping between multiple projects in a fast-paced environment

* You are proficient in Microsoft Office, particularly Excel and PowerPoint

* You can create basic graphics using AI, Canva, or Adobe tools



What you'll do:

* Program and test online surveys in our survey platform, including
 and texting campaigns, logic testing, and distribution setup

* Monitor and manage survey fielding: track quotas, sample quality, response rates, and project timelines

* Coordinate with external vendors - online panel providers, call centers, and mail vendors - and provide regular updates to internal project managers

* Prepare and clean sample files: create organized sample files, generate unique data fields

* Develop mail materials and coordinate with internal team and external mail vendors

* Perform data cleaning on survey outputs to verify data quality and integrity

* Code and analyze open-ended responses

* QC all deliverables: cross-check data sets, verify formatting, catch errors before client delivery

* Build charts, reporting visuals, and basic dashboards in PowerPoint, Canva, or Looker Studio

* Support the research team across the full project lifecycle



What we're NOT looking for:

Someone whose survey experience is limited to academic research or distributing simple online links



Required qualifications:

* At least one year of experience programming and fielding surveys in a professional opinion or market research setting

* Proficiency in online survey programming with a survey platform (tell us which ones you know well)

* Experience managing fielding for market research or public opinion research studies

* Experience with data cleaning and response monitoring

* Strong English writing, proofreading, and communication skills

* Bachelor's degree in marketing, political science, communication, business administration, or a related field



Preferred:

* Experience with SPSS, R, Wincross, or similar statistical/tabulation software



What we provide:

* A collaborative team

* A supportive environment where asking questions is encouraged

* Company-paid PhilHealth or HMO

* 13th month pay

* Paid time off



TO APPLY:

Send a link to your resume and LinkedIn profile. Also tell us:?

1. Which survey platform(s) have you programmed in, and approximately how many surveys have you built?

2. Describe one project where you managed fielding from start to finish - what was the methodology, and what did you personally handle?



Put Survey Fielding Manager in the subject line. Use 5 apply points.

I am Adam Probolsky. I will read your application personally. This position is available immediately.



About Probolsky Research: We are a Latina- and woman-owned market and opinion research firm working for corporate, election, government, and nonprofit clients.

title: Video Editor / YouTube Growth Expert
description:
**Job Description: YouTube Video Editor & Growth Strategist**



We are looking for a talented and driven YouTube Video Editor and Growth Strategist to join our team. This is a dual-role position, and both sides of this role carry equal weight. You must bring proven experience and a strong portfolio in both. If you are passionate about building audiences, obsessed with what makes people click and stay, and know how to make a video feel polished and purposeful, we want to hear from you!



**Responsibility 1: Video Editing**



You will be responsible for editing all YouTube content from raw footage to a fully polished, publish-ready video. This is not basic cutting and trimming. We are looking for someone who understands the psychology of retention, knows how to pace a video to keep viewers watching, and can make a talking-head format feel dynamic and engaging.



Responsibilities include editing raw footage into compelling, well-paced YouTube videos, adding graphics, lower thirds, captions, and b-roll where appropriate, maintaining a consistent visual style that reflects our brand, while creating intro hooks that stop the scroll in the first 30 seconds, and delivering videos on deadline without sacrificing quality. Shorts and editing reel type of content will be needed. 



Requirements include a portfolio of edited YouTube videos with demonstrated audience retention performance, proficiency in Adobe Premiere Pro, Final Cut Pro, or equivalent professional editing software, experience with motion graphics and text animation, and a strong eye for pacing, visual storytelling, and brand consistency.





**Responsibility 2: YouTube Growth Strategy**



Editing a great video is only half the job. You will also own the strategy behind how each video performs. This means you understand how YouTube's algorithm works, how to research what audiences are searching for, and how to position every video for maximum discoverability and click-through.



Responsibilities include conducting keyword and topic research using vidIQ, TubeBuddy, or equivalent tools, writing SEO-optimized titles, descriptions, and tags for every upload, designing or directing high-converting thumbnails that are consistent with channel branding, managing the YouTube channel end-to-end including upload scheduling, playlists, pinned comments, and community posts, monitoring analytics to identify what is working and adjusting strategy accordingly, and developing a content calendar aligned with channel growth goals.



**Responsibility 3: General Video Editing**

Beyond YouTube, you will be responsible for long-form video content that lives on our other learning platforms, including editing them into short-form assets that extend our reach across multiple platforms.





**What We Are Building**



Our content is focused on communication, leadership, and professional skills for ambitious professionals. We are growing intentionally and strategically, and we need someone who can help us get there faster without cutting corners. This role is an opportunity to build something from the ground up with a clear vision, an engaged audience, and a founder who is deeply committed to showing up consistently. We are looking for someone who works hard, meets all deadlines, and communicates proactively. 



About us: we are a female-led business that encourages flexibility and autonomy. We are not looking to micromanage; instead, we want to hire people who are self-motivated and responsible and can deliver projects on time. Success in this role means being proactive and communicative, while having fun along the way.



**To Apply**



Please submit your resume, please follow the instructions carefully. All applicants who don't follow will be voided:



Include a cover letter of why you're the right fit. 

Include a resume with previous job experience.

Include a link to a YouTube channel you have edited and grown. 

Include examples of thumbnails you have designed.

Share a brief note on your approach to growing an early-stage channel.

Include any additional social media videos you have edited (doesn't have to be for YouTube)
