title: Sales Development Representative (SDR)
description:
Work From Home | Night Shift (EST) | Full-Time / Part-Time 



Location: Remote (availability during EST business hours required) 

Employment Type: Full-Time or Part-Time 

Industry: Financial Technology | Online Payments | SaaS



About Us

Zift is a US-based fintech company providing payment solutions for SaaS and software businesses.

We work with international clients and are expanding our sales team.



About the Role

We’re looking for a motivated and confident Sales Development Representative (SDR) to join our growing team.

If you enjoy talking to people, can handle cold calls, and want a stable long-term remote job with a US company — this is for you.

You will be the first point of contact with potential clients and play a key role in growing our business.



What You’ll Do

- Make cold calls to B2B clients

- Briefly present the product and assess client interest

- Schedule qualified appointments (discovery calls and demos) and smoothly hand off interested clients to the Account Executive team.

- Handle objections calmly and professionally

- Achieve daily, weekly, and monthly quotas for outreach (calls and
s) and qualified appointments set.

- Maintain accurate and timely records of all prospecting activities, client interactions, and lead statuses in the CRM system.



Who We’re Looking For

- Strong spoken English (clear and confident)

- Comfortable speaking on the phone for long periods

- Experience in cold calling / sales / appointment setting is a plus

- Friendly, confident, and positive attitude

- Goal-driven and motivated to earn bonuses

- Fast learner and coachable

- Access to a reliable, high-speed internet connection and a dedicated, quiet workspace suitable for professional calls.

- Familiarity with the B2B SaaS, financial technology, or online payments industries is a significant plus.

- Proven ability to work independently and meet quantifiable performance goals.



Work Schedule

- Must be available during US Eastern Time (EST) hours

- This is a night shift role (Philippine time)



Compensation & Benefits

- Competitive base salary 

- Performance bonuses 

- 100% remote work (no commute)

- Work with a US fintech company 

- Supportive and friendly team



How to Apply:

Send your resume along with a short video or voice introduction (1–2 minutes). For your video or voice introduction, please briefly describe your cold-calling experience and provide a mini-pitch for a SaaS product (1-2 minutes total).

The hiring process may include a mock cold call and a written cold
 exercise, which are common assessment signals for the SDR role.

title: Social Media Content Creator
description:
This Role Is For You If You’re:



- Chronically online (and proud of it)

- Always scrolling, saving, reposting, analyzing

- The friend who just knows why a post went viral

- Fluent in English and confident expressing ideas

- Creative, enthusiastic, and full of good energy

- Lowkey building (or dreaming of building) your own online brand



If social media feels less like “work” and more like second nature, then this is for you! 



What You’ll Be Doing:



You’ll help create, shape, and manage content that’s meant to perform, not just exist.



Your day-to-day includes:

- Creating short-form videos and trendy visual content

- Brainstorming ideas based on what’s viral, aesthetic, and engaging

- Writing captions that sound human (not corporate)

- Staying on top of trends, audios, formats, and Gen Z humor

- Helping grow social media pages through smart, creative content

- Collaborating with a creative team that actually values your ideas



You’ll be trained, supported, and encouraged to bring your own creative voice.





What We’re Looking For:



We’re not obsessed with years of experience. We care more about:



- Genuine love for social media + content creation

- Strong understanding of Gen Z trends and internet culture

- High-level English communication skills

- A good eye for aesthetics and visuals

- Confidence, enthusiasm, and positive energy

- Willingness to learn, experiment, and grow

- Having a personal brand or active social media presence is a bonus, not a requirement.





Why You’ll Love This Role:



- 100% remote (work from anywhere)

- No micromanaging

- Paid training + continuous feedback

- A creative, supportive, non-toxic team

- Opportunities to grow into higher-level creative or strategy roles

- You get paid to do what you already love





How to Apply:



- Send us a short video introduction (1–2 minutes) showing your personality, creativity, and why you’d be a great fit

- Links to your creative portfolio ot any content you’ve created (personal or professional)

- Link to your resume/CV



IMPORTANT:

1. No video intro = not considered

2. Boring or low-effort intro = also a pass

3. Type '67' in your subject line so we know you've read this far



This role is for someone who shows up with personality, creativity, and confidence.



If you read this and thought “wait… this is literally me” — apply now.

We can’t wait to see what you bring!

title: Shopify Store Developer
description:
Job Overview

We are an e-commerce company in USA and looking for a part-time Shopify Developer to help maintain, improve, and upgrade our existing Shopify store.

This role focuses on ongoing theme customization, performance optimization, and upgrading our current theme to the latest Horizon version, ensuring a smooth and high-converting storefront experience.

You will work closely with our team to continuously improve the storefront while supporting day-to-day updates and fixes.



Key Responsibilities

• Maintain and customize our existing Shopify Vision theme (Liquid, HTML, CSS, JavaScript)

• Implement new features, sections, and UI improvements based on business needs

• Upgrade current theme to the latest Horizon version, ensuring compatibility and stability

• Troubleshoot and fix bugs, layout issues, and app conflicts

• Optimize storefront performance (page speed, mobile responsiveness, UX)

• Ensure cross-browser and cross-device compatibility

• Collaborate with internal team to implement updates, campaigns, and improvements

• Recommend improvements to enhance conversion rate and user experience



Requirements

• Proven experience with Shopify theme development and customization, with hands-on experience working on Vision theme projects

• Strong knowledge of Liquid, HTML, CSS, JavaScript

• Experience maintaining and improving existing Shopify stores (not just building from scratch)

• Familiarity with Shopify 2.0 / Horizon themes

• Ability to troubleshoot Shopify themes, apps, and compatibility issues

• Understanding of e-commerce best practices (UX, conversion, performance)

• Ability to work independently and manage tasks in a part-time setup

• Good communication skills in English



Nice to Have

• Experience with Shopify theme upgrades or migrations

• Experience with Shopify apps and third-party integrations

• Basic understanding of SEO and site performance optimization

• Experience working with Git or version control



Work Details

• Part-time (flexible hours)

• Remote work

• Ongoing work with potential for long-term collaboration

• Workload mainly includes maintenance, improvements, and theme upgrades



How to Apply

Please include:

• Examples/Links of Shopify stores you have worked on (especially theme customization or optimization)

• Brief description of your experience with Shopify, including the themes you have worked with

• A short description of how you have collaborated with teams or clients in previous projects (e.g., communication style, tools used, workflow)



Applications without proof of Shopify experience will be ignored.

title: Amazon PPC Specialist
description:
We’re a fast-growing e-commerce company with a home decor brand, marketing in Amazon marketplace. Our mission is to create thoughtfully designed products that families love and trust — and we’re looking for a skilled Amazon PPC Specialist to help scale our reach and performance.



Role Overview



You’ll take ownership of our Amazon advertising strategy — from campaign setup and optimization to analytics and reporting. You’ll be responsible for maximizing ROAS, driving growth, and managing budgets efficiently across multiple marketplaces (US, CA, AU, EU)



This role is ideal for someone who thrives in a data-driven environment, understands Amazon’s evolving ecosystem, and can collaborate closely with the marketing, operations, and inventory teams to align ad spend with stock levels and sales goals.



Key Responsibilities



1. Develop, manage, and optimize Amazon PPC campaigns (Sponsored Products, Sponsored Brands, Sponsored Display).

2. Analyze keyword performance, search term data, and ACoS to continuously refine campaigns.

3. Monitor daily budgets, bids, and campaign pacing to ensure profitability and scalability.

4. Provide daily, weekly and monthly performance reports with actionable insights.

5. Stay ahead of Amazon algorithm updates, new ad features, and best practices.



Requirements



Proven experience managing Amazon PPC campaigns (2–3 years).

Strong understanding of Amazon Seller Central, advertising dashboards, and reporting tools.

Proficiency with keyword research, bid optimization, and data analytics.

Highly analytical with strong Excel/Google Sheets skills.

Excellent communication and time management skills.

Preferably someone who is proficient in Scale Insights



Bonus Points



Experience in inventory management, demand forecasting, or stock allocation.

Prior experience working at or with an Amazon-focused agency such as My Amazon Guy or similar.

Familiarity with tools such as Helium 10, DataDive, Sellersprite, Keepa, Scale Insights

Experience managing campaigns across international marketplaces.



Why Join Us



Be part of a fast-scaling e-commerce brand with strong growth 
ntum.

Work directly with a passionate, hands-on leadership team.

Flexible working hours and remote-friendly setup.



Opportunity to grow into a senior marketing or operations hybrid role.



Include 'YES' in cover letter

title: Graphic Designer (Junior–Mid Level Welcome) — Print Production & Layout Systems
description:
Remote Graphic Designer — Print Production & Layout Systems ($3.50/hr Trial $3.75–$4.00/hr Long-Term)



I’m building a structured, scalable print production environment across multiple brands and looking for a graphic designer who enjoys creating designs that go directly into real-world printing and production.



This role combines creative design with production-ready layout work. Your designs will move directly from Adobe Creative Cloud into print, so understanding print setup such as bleed, crop marks, safe margins, and layout preparation is important.



We are looking for someone who enjoys both design and structured workflow systems, helping build templates and repeatable processes that make production faster and more efficient.



This is a stable, long-term role for someone who wants consistent work inside a structured environment.



This is not a one-off freelance project, this is a long-term internal operational role.



All business workflows and infrastructure details will be shared after NDA and hiring.



What You’ll Be Doing (Examples Only — Not Limited To):



• Create graphic designs for print products (business cards, flyers, postcards, signage, etc.)

• Deliver print-ready files with proper bleed, crop marks, safe margins, and correct sizing

• Understand and work with standard sheet sizes (8.5x11, 11x17, 12x18, 13x19, etc.)

• Build multi-up layouts (step & repeat / imposition layouts when required)

• Create and maintain reusable production templates

• Export production-ready PDFs optimized for printing

• Maintain organized file naming and folder structure

• Support fast-turnaround print production workflow



You’re a Good Fit If:



• Strong experience with Adobe Creative Cloud (Illustrator, InDesign, Photoshop)

• Experience preparing files for print production

• Understand bleed, trim, gutters, crop marks, and safe zones

• Comfortable creating layouts that are immediately ready for printing

• Enjoy structured workflows and repeatable systems

• Fast responder and organized communicator

• Experience working in a print shop or production environment is a major plus



Junior–mid level candidates welcome if you are reliable and committed to learning structured workflows.



Work Style & Communication Expectations:



• Fast response communication during working hours is required

• Work inside structured workflows and template-based systems

• Reliability, speed, and consistency are prioritized over creative experimentation

• Daily workflow visibility and responsiveness are essential



Privacy & NDA Terms



• Signed NDA required before access

• Internal operational role (not client-facing)

• Trusted with private workflows and infrastructure

• No agencies or subcontracting



Minimum Requirements — Do Not Apply If:



• You primarily use Canva or beginner design tools

• You do not understand print-ready file setup

• You want short-term freelance projects only

• You require daily step-by-step supervision



Pay & Commitment:



• $3.50/hr — trial period

• Increase to $3.50–$4.00/hr based on speed, consistency, and ownership after trial

• Paid weekly initially ? bi-weekly after onboarding

• Long-term growth opportunity as production systems expand



Schedule (REQUIRED):



• Monday – Friday

• Work hours between 9:30 AM – 4:00 PM PST

• Approximately 25–35 hours per week

• This is a dedicated working schedule — not flexible freelance hours



HOW TO APPLY:

Copy and paste:



“Graphic Designer — [Your First Name]”



Include:

• Resume (required)

• Short introduction (2–5 sentences)

• Weekly availability + timezone

• Confirmation you accept rate + NDA

• Confirmation you can work within required schedule window

• Portfolio showing PRINT-READY design work (REQUIRED)

• One example where you prepared a file for printing



Applications missing required information will not be reviewed.



If you’re reliable, structured, and understand both design and print production — let’s build.



Blue River Management

title: Video editor for FB ads

Our founder Joshua Iyalla holds three Guinness World Records, including the world's fastest puncher. 



Joshua and Joel are full-time content creators with over 10 years of combined experience in the industry and a combined following of 3.5 million across social media. They understand content at a deep level and know exactly what they want. They need an editor who shares that same understanding of what makes content hit — someone who can take their vision and bring it to life on screen with precision and creativity.

 ad creatives using raw footage we provide

Parallel run

 / C# backend experience

title: Architect (2D/3D Design & Planning) | Remote | UK & Ireland Projects
description:
Please follow the application instructions below.



About PropFix.ie



PropFix.ie is Ireland's trusted property maintenance specialist, offering comprehensive solutions with years of expertise across the construction and renovation sectors. We transform spaces through our diverse range of services, from handyman solutions and office fit-outs to complete renovations, house extensions, and specialized installations including doors, floors, and partitions.



Our commitment to delivering the best value property maintenance has established us as a reliable partner for residential and commercial clients nationwide. With decades of experience, including 50 years of expertise in garage conversions, we pride ourselves on impeccable craftsmanship, turning our clients' visions into reality while ensuring their properties remain in top condition.



Role Overview

We are seeking a skilled and versatile Architect Virtual Assistant to join our dynamic team. This multifaceted role combines architectural design expertise with comprehensive project support and administrative management. The successful candidate will provide essential drafting services, create detailed 3D models and visualizations, while also managing project coordination, supplier relationships, and administrative tasks to ensure seamless project delivery from concept to completion.



Key Responsibilities

Project Management & Coordination



Project Scheduling: Manage project timelines, milestones, and delivery schedules

Team Management: Coordinate and oversee subcontractors and in-house employees

Resource Planning: Coordinate project resources and workflow optimization.



Supplier & Procurement Management



Supplier Coordination: Manage relationships with suppliers and vendors

Procurement Support: Assist with material sourcing, ordering, and delivery coordination

Cost Management: Support procurement processes and maintain supplier databases

Quality Control: Ensure materials and services meet project specifications and standards



Administrative & CRM Support



CRM Management: Maintain and update customer relationship management systems with project and client information

 Management: Handle professional
 correspondence and client communications

General Administration: Perform various administrative tasks to support daily operations

Ad Hoc Tasks: Flexibly handle diverse project-related tasks as required by the business



Required Skills & Experience

Technical Requirements



CAD Proficiency: Advanced skills in Archicad, Revit, AutoCAD, and SketchUp for architectural design and 3D modeling

Architectural Knowledge: Strong understanding of architectural principles, construction methods, and building standards

Visualization Skills: Experience creating 3D renderings, video walkthroughs, and presentation materials

Technical Drawing: Proficiency in producing detailed construction drawings and technical specifications



Project Management Skills



Coordination Experience: Proven ability to manage multiple projects and coordinate with various stakeholders

Team Leadership: Experience managing subcontractors, suppliers, and tea
mbers

Scheduling: Strong organizational skills with experience in project scheduling and timeline management

Problem-Solving: Analytical thinking and ability to resolve project challenges efficiently



Administrative & Technology Skills



CRM Systems: Experience with customer relationship management platforms and databases

Communication: Excellent written and verbal communication skills for client and contractor liaison

Software Proficiency: Competency in Microsoft Office Suite, project management software, and administrative tools

Multitasking: Ability to handle diverse tasks and adapt to changing project requirements



Professional Qualifications



Education: Degree in Architecture, Engineering, Construction Management, or related field preferred

Industry Experience: Previous experience in architectural practice, construction, or property development

Virtual Assistance: Experience working remotely and managing virtual project coordination



What We Offer



Competitive salary package

Opportunity to work on diverse architectural and construction projects

Professional development and training opportunities

Collaborative and supportive remote work environment

Career growth potential within an expanding company

Involvement in challenging projects across Ireland's property maintenance and construction sector

Remote working flexibility with comprehensive project support systems

title: Youtube Video Editor
description:
Job Description:

We are looking for a talented and detail-oriented video editor to help create high-quality, engaging videos. The ideal candidate should have experience working with raw footage from phone recordings and be able to enhance content through expert editing, seamless transitions, and creative additions.



What You'll Do:

1. Tailored Workflow & Enhanced Quality: Optimize raw footage with noise reduction, audio leveling, and effects to elevate overall content quality.

2. Precise Editing & Efficiency: Edit and enhance videos while ensuring quick turnaround times without compromising quality.

3. Professional Video Editing: Deliver precise and creative edits to match the project’s needs.

4. Post-Production: Provide comprehensive post-production services to ensure polished, professional results.

5. Color Correction: Apply color grading and correction to enhance the visual appeal of videos.

6. Sound Design: Add background music, improve audio quality, and create an immersive audio experience.

7. Creative Storytelling: Make decisions on where to insert B-rolls, animations, zoom transitions, and graphics to make videos more engaging.

8. Subtitles & Captions: Add subtitles for accessibility and engagement.

9. Organizing Clips & Footage: Structure videos from beginning to end with a seamless flow.

10. Transitions & Video Effects: Implement smooth transitions and effects for a professional touch.

11. Intro & Outro: Create engaging intros and outros to enhance brand identity.

12. Multiple Revisions: Open to feedback and revisions to achieve the desired final product.



Ideal Candidate:



- Experienced in editing phone-recorded content

- Creative and detail-oriented

- Understands sound design and background music selection

- Proficient in color grading and correction

- Skilled in using professional video editing software (Premiere Pro, DaVinci Resolve, Final Cut Pro, etc.)

- Efficient and deadline-driven



If you're passionate about video editing and can transform raw footage into engaging, high-quality content, we'd love to hear from you!



To apply, please send:

1. Examples of past video editing work

2. Are you willing to sign an NDA before getting the materials to edit?

3. Your typical turnaround time for a 3-5 minute video

4. Are you willing to create a short sample? We will provide raw footage for the test.

title: Drainage Design Engineer (Civil 3D / Revit)
description:
About Us

Water Design Engineers is an independent engineering consultancy focused on solving water in the landscape. Our work includes drainage and SuDS design, irrigation design, water feature design, constructed wetlands, water treatment coordination, and wider water infrastructure input across a range of projects.

We work closely with landscape architects, architects, developers, and other consultants to deliver practical, coordinated, technically robust design solutions.

 

The Role

We are looking for a Drainage and Water Infrastructure Design Engineer to join our team. Drainage will be the primary focus of the role, so we are looking for someone with solid experience in drainage and SuDS design who can contribute confidently to live projects from the outset.

This role is not limited to traditional drainage work. You will also support the wider team on projects involving water infrastructure, landscape-led water management, wetlands, irrigation interfaces, and related civil design coordination. We need someone who is technically capable, practical in their thinking, and willing to get involved wherever needed to help move projects forward.

We are ideally looking for someone with around 4 to 7 years of relevant experience, although strong applicants outside this range will also be considered where they can demonstrate the right technical capability, software skills, and attitude.

 

Key Responsibilities

Undertake drainage and SuDS design as the primary part of the role

Prepare drawings, design information, calculations, and technical support documents

Develop coordinated drainage layouts and levels-based solutions from available survey and design information

Use Civil 3D for drainage-related design and modelling work

Work in Revit to support model-based project coordination and design development

Coordinate with internal and external project teams including landscape architects, architects, and other consultants

Support wider water infrastructure and civil design elements where required across the company’s project portfolio

Identify technical risks, clashes, missing information, and design dependencies early

Contribute to a practical, team-focused design environment with a positive and proactive attitude

 

What We’re Looking For

Degree in Civil Engineering or similar relevant discipline

Strong drainage design experience, ideally gained over around 4 to 7 years in practice

Good working knowledge of SuDS and or external works drainage design

Proficient in Civil 3D

Able to work in Revit for design coordination

Good understanding of levels, flow paths, site constraints, and buildable design solutions

Strong communication skills and a practical, solutions-led mindset

Someone with a can-do attitude who is willing to contribute to a range of project types, not just stay in a narrow discipline box

 

Desirable

Experience working on landscape-led or multidisciplinary projects

Experience supporting drainage strategies, flood-related infrastructure, or wider water management design

Understanding of how drainage interfaces with broader external works and water infrastructure systems

 

Why Join Us

This is an opportunity to join a growing consultancy with a strong technical focus and a clear specialism in water-related design. You will be part of a team working on varied and interesting projects, with the chance to contribute beyond standard drainage design and help shape integrated water solutions across the landscape.

 

To Apply

Please send your CV at 
 and a short covering note outlining your relevant experience.

title: Copywriter (Sales & Conversion Focus)
description:
We are looking for an experienced Senior Direct Response Copywriter with C2-level English and proven sales / conversion-focused copywriting experience. This is not a general content writer role. You must understand how to write copy that sells, converts, and drives measurable action.



You will be responsible for writing high-converting short-form copy used across our remote team’s messaging and content systems. The ideal candidate has experience in chat-based sales,
 funnels, retention, upsell, and objection-handling copy, not just blog-style writing.



This role is for someone who can work independently, think strategically, and optimize copy based on conversion signals (not just aesthetics). If you only write for SEO, blogs, or social media posts and don’t have a background in performance-driven, sales-oriented copywriting, this role is not for you.



Responsibilities:

 • Write persuasive, short-form sales copy for messaging, scripts, captions, and promotional content

 • Create conversion-focused messages with strong hooks, urgency, scarcity, and clear CTAs

 • Write direct response copy for warm audiences, retention, upsells, and cross-sells

 • Develop copy angles and frameworks that drive engagement and purchases

 • Adjust tone and style to match different brand personalities

 • Work with limited supervision and high trust

 • Test and refine copy based on performance and feedback



Requirements (Must Have):

 • C2-level English (near-native fluency required)

 • Proven experience in direct response copywriting or sales copywriting

 • Demonstrable experience in sales, conversion, or performance marketing (not just generic content writing)

 • Strong understanding of buyer psychology, hooks, urgency, scarcity, CTAs, and objection handling

 • Experience in chat-based sales,
 funnels, e-commerce copy, or lead-generation campaigns is a strong advantage.

 • Ability to write clear, persuasive, and emotionally effective English copy quickly

 • High attention to detail, reliability, and professionalism

 • Independent, proactive work style – no need for constant supervision



Preferred but not mandatory:

 • Experience with subscription services, membership models, or recurring revenue systems

 • Experience in chat-based sales, customer retention, or high-ticket sales



Application Instructions:

 1. Write a short message on
 to this
 

 • Include: “Direct Response Copywriter Application – Your Name” and a 1–2 sentence introduction about yourself.?(Only applications that include this
 message will be considered. This is a simple filter to see if you read the instructions carefully.)

 2. Send your CV / Resume via
. Keep it short and focused on sales, copywriting, or performance-related experience.

 3. Send 2 writing samples that show your direct response or sales copywriting skills (not generic blog posts or academic text).

 4. Write a short answer to this question:?“What makes a piece of copy truly convert, and not just read well?”

 5. Write a 3–5 sentence persuasive sales message targeting a warm audience who has shown interest but has not yet purchased. Show how you use urgency, curiosity, and a clear CTA.



Important:

We are only looking for writers who understand copywriting as a sales and conversion tool, not just as a creative writing job. If you do not have demonstrable sales / direct response / conversion copywriting experience or if your English is not at C2 level, please do not apply.

The Role



We're hiring a Vibecoder to automate our entire eCommerce operation — from product listing and inventory management to pricing strategies, competitor monitoring, order processing, and reporting.



You'll use AI-powered coding tools (Cursor, Claude, GitHub Copilot, etc.) to rapidly build custom bots, scrapers, dashboards, and automations that replace manual workflows and scale our business. You're not just writing code — you're engineering systems that run our commerce on autopilot.

mber

title: Prop Firm Data Analyst (AI Database Manager)
description:
Help maintain the database of the most accurate prop firm AI platform in the trading industry.



Prop Firm Stash Corp. is building StashAI, an AI-powered platform that helps traders understand proprietary trading firm rules, compare accounts, and make better funding decisions. Our platform aggregates data from multiple prop firms and converts it into a structured database that powers our AI assistant. Because prop firm rules frequently change, we are looking for someone who will help maintain the accuracy of our rule database and keep StashAI up to date.



Job Description:

- We are looking for a detail-oriented research analyst who will monitor proprietary trading firms daily and update our internal database when rules change.

- Your work will ensure StashAI always provides accurate and reliable answers to traders.



Your daily responsibilities would include:

- Monitor prop firm websites, FAQs, 
 announcements, and newsletters for rule changes.

- Identify updates to evaluation rules, payout policies, drawdown structures, and account requirements.

- Update the StashAI structured rule database when changes occur.

- Verify information across multiple sources before publishing updates.

- Maintain a change log documenting all updates to prop firm rules.



To qualify for the position, you should have experience in at least one of the following:

- Data research or financial research

- Working with spreadsheets or structured databases

- Knowledge of proprietary trading firms

- Experience documenting processes or policies



We work in a fast-moving industry, so it’s important that you are:

- Extremely detail-oriented

- A fast learner

- Self-motivated and able to work independently

- Analytical and structured in your thinking

- Comfortable researching financial platforms



You should be comfortable using:

- Google Docs / Google Sheets

- Excel or other spreadsheet tools

- Web research using search engines

- Communication tools (Slack, 
, etc.)

- Project management tools (Notion, Trello, Asana)



Desired Skills (Nice to Have):

- Experience trading futures or prop firm accounts

- Familiarity with prop firms like Apex, Topstep, MyFundedFutures, etc.

- Experience working with AI knowledge bases or data labeling



Work Schedule:

- Flexible hours.



Salary:

- Negotiable depending on experience.



How to Apply:

- When applying, include the phrase “Prop Firm Data Analyst” in the subject line.

title: Shopify Developer
description:
The Shopify Developer will be responsible for developing, customizing, and maintaining Shopify stores to ensure optimal functionality, performance, and user experience. This role focuses on building and modifying Shopify themes, implementing custom features, integrating third-party tools, and troubleshooting technical issues.



You will collaborate closely with designers, marketers, and eCommerce teams to deliver scalable solutions that improve store performance, streamline operations, and enhance the customer shopping experience. The ideal candidate has strong experience in Shopify development, Liquid templating, and front-end technologies.



Key Responsibilities:

- Develop, customize, and maintain Shopify themes using Liquid, HTML, CSS, and JavaScript

- Develop and manage Shopify API integrations with third-party tools, apps, and external platforms

- Create and maintain custom sections, templates, and reusable theme components

- Implement conversion-focused UI/UX improvements and support A/B testing initiatives

- Manage and configure Shopify apps, plugins, and integrations

- Support store migrations, theme upgrades, and feature deployments

- Ensure code quality, scalability, and maintainability through proper documentation and best practices

Qualifications:

- Experience building and customizing Shopify themes and storefronts

- Knowledge of Shopify APIs, webhooks, and third-party integrations

title: Creative Generalist & Creative Strategist
description:
CREATIVE GENERALIST



We’re hiring a Creative Generalist with strong design and video editing chops to produce compelling visual assets for our paid media campaigns, landing pages, and brand communication. This hybrid role supports 2–3 DTC brands to start and is perfect for someone who thrives on fast-paced creative production, balancing aesthetics with performance goals. You’ll be responsible for creating everything from static ads and animated social assets to UGC edits and conversion-driven landing page graphics.



Key Responsibilities:

- Design static, motion, and video ad creatives for Meta, TikTok, and Google

- Edit high-performing UGC ads with strong hooks, CTAs, captions, and transitions

- Repurpose testimonials, reviews, and influencer content for paid and organic use

- Create
, SMS, and promotional visuals aligned with brand identity

- Stylize social proof assets (e.g., screenshots, quotes) into branded, engaging formats

- Follow platform-specific best practices for ad creatives

- Ensure design supports performance goals like ROAS and CAC

- Maintain brand consistency across diverse formats and placements

- Collaborate with media buyers, marketers, and creatives to meet campaign goals

- Stay current with trends in ad design and DTC creative performance



Qualifications:

- At least 2 years of experience in graphic design and video editing for digital marketing.

- Advanced skills in Adobe Creative Suite (Photoshop, Illustrator, Premiere Pro, After Effects).

- Proven experience creating high-converting static and motion ads for Meta, TikTok, or Google.

- Strong eye for layout, color, typography, and brand consistency.

- Experience editing UGC and social proof content for performance-driven campaigns.

- Able to follow creative briefs, meet tight deadlines, and collaborate well with remote teams.

- Portfolio demonstrating versatility across static design, motion, and video editing.





-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------





CREATIVE STRATEGIST



The Creative Strategist will own the research, ideation, and optimization of creative assets across paid ads,
/SMS, landing pages, and UGC. They'll translate brand goals, market data, and consumer psychology into briefs, scripts, and testing roadmaps, partnering with designers, editors, media buyers, and UGC creators to execute.



This role focuses on conversion optimization and creative direction — guiding creative generalists, reviewing and approving output, and ensuring all creatives are aligned with CRO goals. The strategist does not handle direct web design, but must have strong CRO experience to boost campaign effectiveness.



Key Responsibilities:

- Analyze client creative performance, benchmark competitors, and identify positioning and messaging opportunities across the full funnel.

- Develop creative roadmaps for DTC brands, concept new hooks and angles, and build testing frameworks across multiple ad formats.

- Create detailed UGC briefs, collaborate with creators through production, and curate social proof for ads and owned channels.

- Partner with media buyers and cross-functional teams to ensure creative aligns with funnel stages, KPIs, and brand standards.

- Guide creative generalists and support
/SMS and CRO-focused landing page strategy without executing web design.

- Track and interpret performance data, document insights, and iterate continuously to optimize campaigns.

- Present strategies, testing plans, and performance recaps to clients and internal leadership to support retention and revenue goals.



Qualifications:     

- Proven experience in Conversion Rate Optimization (CRO) and creative strategy for digital marketing.

- Ability to analyze creative performance metrics and translate data insights into actionable design or messaging improvements.

- Strong leadership and feedback skills to guide generalists and align creative output with performance goals.

- Ability to drive creative decisions that enhance campaign effectiveness and conversion rates.



What We Offer:

- Permanent remote setup

- Unlimited free access to MAG School courses and SOP Library

- Opportunities for professional development and career advancement
